lede: A look at October 12 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Pedro I of Brazil and Bode Miller, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Collapse of the Space Building
events.desc: An apartment building collapse in Medellín, Colombia results in the deaths of twelve people.

events.title: Provisional Irish Republican Army
events.desc: The Provisional Irish Republican Army fail to assassinate Prime Minister Margaret Thatcher and her cabinet. The bomb kills five people and wounds at least 31 others.

events.title: Typhoon Tip
events.desc: Typhoon Tip becomes the largest and most intense tropical cyclone ever recorded.

events.title: Nikita Khrushchev
events.desc: Soviet Premier Nikita Khrushchev pounds his shoe on a desk at the United Nations to protest a Philippine assertion.

events.title: Iron lung
events.desc: An iron lung respirator is used for the first time at Boston Children's Hospital.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: British nurse Edith Cavell is executed by a German firing squad for helping Allied soldiers escape from occupied Belgium.

events.title: Pledge of Allegiance
events.desc: The Pledge of Allegiance is first recited by students in many US public schools.

events.title: Jeanne Geneviève Garnerin
events.desc: Jeanne Geneviève Labrosse becomes the first woman to jump from a balloon with a parachute.

events.title: Peasants' War (1798)
events.desc: Flemish and Luxembourgish peasants launch the rebellion against French rule known as the Peasants' War.

births.name: August Horch
births.line: German engineer and businessman, founded Audi

deaths.text: Alex Salmond, Scottish economist and politician, First Minister of Scotland
Dennis Ritchie, American computer scientist, created the C programming language
Anatole France, French journalist, novelist, and poet, Nobel Prize laureate
Piero della Francesca, Italian mathematician and painter

observances.text: Christian feast day: Edith Cavell and Elizabeth Fry (Church of England)
Christian feast day: Our Lady of the Pillar (Fiestas del Pilar)
Christian feast day: Our Lady of Aparecida
